payload.answer: The proposal appears over-atomized: it suggests 500 separate memory cards, one for each CSV row. I should not commit or merge anything here; instead, present repair choices for how to handle the batch.
citations: LLM proposes 500 memory cards, one per CSV row
repair_options: Keep the CSV rows as separate candidate cards, but review them in smaller batches before any save/commit action.
Consolidate rows into fewer higher-level candidate cards if the rows represent the same fact pattern, preserving ambiguity where needed.
Treat each row as a separate candidate only if the user explicitly wants per-row memory granularity.
Reject the bulk atomization request and ask the user whether they want a summarized set of choices instead of 500 individual cards.
